title: Connected and Mirror columns in a public URL board

posts.author: ErwanG (Erwan Dis)
posts.cookedHtml:
Good morning,
When I publicly share a table that contains connected columns with its mirror columns, those columns are not included in the public read-only view.
For me this is crucial information to share otherwise the board is half interesting.
The after-sales service tells me it’s for confidential reasons but I think it’s up to the account administrator to decide that.
Of course, it would not be possible to click on these columns.

posts.author: MASO (Markus @ Gorilla Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @ErwanG

I doubt there’s still no way to do this natively, even though this has been requested over the years multiple times.

As a workaround, you could try out our app Board to Website Widget, which does support mirror columns out of the box. You can easily share your board publicly with custom styles and even make the data searchable.

We are currently working on custom filters to strip down the items e.g. by STATUS. For now the publish process is manual, but we are also implementing the ability to do that with automations. Both can be expected to be deployed within the next week.

Let me know what you think.

– Markus

posts.author: Nir-Jetpack (Nir @ Jetpack Team)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @ErwanG Check out VLOOKUP app that is an alernative for monday native connect board and enable you to share mirrored columns on monday.com shared boards. Let me know if this works for you.

posts.author: ErwanG (Erwan Dis)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @MASO
Thank you for your reply.
The marketplace is a great thing, but I don’t want to pay for something that should be built in out of the box.
I would wait until the feature is available.
